title: To Thomas Butts
content:
آ آ آ آ To my friend Butts I write
آ آ آ آ My first vision of light,
آ آ آ آ On the yellow sands sitting.
آ آ آ آ The sun was emitting
آ آ آ آ His glorious beams
آ آ آ آ From Heavenï؟½s high streams.
آ آ آ آ Over sea, over land,
آ آ آ آ My eyes did expand
آ آ آ آ Into regions of air,
آ آ آ آ Away from all care;
آ آ آ آ Into regions of fire,
آ آ آ آ Remote from desire;
آ آ آ آ The light of the morning
آ آ آ آ Heavenï؟½s mountains adorning:
آ آ آ آ In particles bright,
آ آ آ آ The jewels of light
آ آ آ آ Distinct shone and clear.
آ آ آ آ Amazï؟½d and in fear
آ آ آ آ I each particle gazï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ Astonishï؟½d, amazï؟½d;
آ آ آ آ For each was a Man
آ آ آ آ Human-formï؟½d. Swift I ran,
آ آ آ آ For they beckonï؟½d to me,
آ آ آ آ Remote by the sea,
آ آ آ آ Saying: ï؟½Each grain of sand,
آ آ آ آ Every stone on the land,
آ آ آ آ Each rock and each hill,
آ آ آ آ Each fountain and rill,
آ آ آ آ Each herb and each tree,
آ آ آ آ Mountain, hill, earth, and sea,
آ آ آ آ Cloud, meteor, and star,
آ آ آ آ Are men seen afar.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ I stood in the streams
آ آ آ آ Of Heavenï؟½s bright beams,
آ آ آ آ And saw Felpham sweet
آ آ آ آ Beneath my bright feet,
آ آ آ آ In soft Female charms;
آ آ آ آ And in her fair arms
آ آ آ آ My Shadow I knew,
آ آ آ آ And my wifeï؟½s Shadow too,
آ آ آ آ And my sister, and friend.
آ آ آ آ We like infants descend
آ آ آ آ In our Shadows on earth,
آ آ آ آ Like a weak mortal birth.
آ آ آ آ My eyes, more and more,
آ آ آ آ Like a sea without shore,
آ آ آ آ Continue expanding,
آ آ آ آ The Heavens commanding;
آ آ آ آ Till the jewels of light,
آ آ آ آ Heavenly men beaming bright,
آ آ آ آ Appearï؟½d as One Man,
آ آ آ آ Who complacent began
آ آ آ آ My limbs to enfold
آ آ آ آ In His beams of bright gold;
آ آ آ آ Like dross purgï؟½d away
آ آ آ آ All my mire and my clay.
آ آ آ آ Soft consumï؟½d in delight,
آ آ آ آ In His bosom sun-bright
آ آ آ آ I remainï؟½d. Soft He smilï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And I heard His voice mild,
آ آ آ آ Saying: ï؟½This is My fold,
آ آ آ آ O thou ram hornï؟½d with gold,
آ آ آ آ Who awakest from sleep
آ آ آ آ On the sides of the deep.
آ آ آ آ On the mountains around
آ آ آ آ The roarings resound
آ آ آ آ Of the lion and wolf,
آ آ آ آ The loud sea, and deep gulf.
آ آ آ آ These are guards of My fold,
آ آ آ آ O thou ram hornï؟½d with gold!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ And the voice faded mild;
آ آ آ آ I remainï؟½d as a child;
آ آ آ آ All I ever had known
آ آ آ آ Before me bright shone:
آ آ آ آ I saw you and your wife
آ آ آ آ By the fountains of life.
آ آ آ آ Such the vision to me
آ آ آ آ Appearï؟½d on the sea.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Blake delivers a powerful performance in "To Thomas Butts"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
